intro: The most concrete split between Orb and Lago is the pricing model: Orb runs on usage-based pricing from $1, while Lago uses freemium with paid plans from $1. That shapes how predictable your monthly spend is and how much of the cost sits in infrastructure versus per-seat or per-event fees. Beyond the headline, Orb carries medium lock-in (billing data exportable) and usage-based pricing from $1; Lago carries low lock-in (open-source billing, self-hostable) and freemium with paid plans from $1. Orb is a fit when you are saas. Lago is a fit when you are saas. The honest trade-off: Orb is explicitly not for simple projects; Lago is explicitly not for simple projects. On the product side, Orb is described as Developer-first subscription and usage billing platform with a flexible pricing model API and Stripe-compatible webhooks, and Lago is described as Open-source metering and usage-based billing API supporting flat, tiered, volume, and percentage pricing models.
quick_take: Orb is for saas; Lago is for saas; decide on pricing model.
verdict_a: Choose Orb if its usage pricing model fits your budget shape better than Lago's freemium model, and you are saas.
verdict_b: Choose Lago if you need a free tier to start before committing budget, and your use case matches saas.

faq.a: Orb starts at $1; Lago starts at $1. On entry price alone that is the comparison, but real cost scales with users, events or volume, which the entry prices do not capture. Check each vendor's full tier list before committing.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Orb to Lago?
faq.a: Migration from Orb to Lago is feasible but not trivial: Orb has medium lock-in (billing data exportable). Core data can move; integrations and workflow logic need rebuild. Allow for a parallel-run period.

On our scoring Orb rates 5/5 for developer experience versus 4/5 for Lago. That suggests day-to-day workflows (CLI, SDKs, docs) are smoother in Orb. Scores are directional; run a short spike on your own stack to confirm.

faq.q: Is Lago a good alternative to Orb?
faq.a: Lago targets the same best-fit audience as Orb (saas). Note that Lago is explicitly not for simple projects, so it only replaces Orb if you are outside that exclusion. The lock-in profile differs (medium vs low), which is the main thing to weigh.
